content: Just as the human brain uses active memory processes to prioritize encoding unexpected, curiosity-sparking information over predictable familiar stimuli, artificial curiosity-driven RL agents (like those using ICM or RND) can be re-designed to adopt this brain mechanism: instead of assigning intrinsic reward to all prediction errors, reward is only allocated to prediction errors of memories that the agent actively selects for reactivation during episodic planning. This creates a self-reinforcing cycle where active memory selection amplifies useful curiosity, cutting down on noisy exploration from trivial unexpected events.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
memory as active learning human brain mechanisms
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
